content.full_text:
Farn Pourrcal PnacrrcEs CouvrrssroN
428 J Street . Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
June 15,2005
Heather C. Mclaughlin
CityAttomey
The City of Benicia
City Hall
250East L Street
Benicia, CA945l0
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. .4.-05-041
Dear Ms. Mclaughlin:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Benicia Mayor
Steve Messina, Vice-MayorElizabethPatterson, and Councilmember Tom Campbell
regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act').r
This advice is based on the facts you have provided in your request. The Fair Political
Practices Commission ("Commission') does not act as a finder of fact when it renders
advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71; Section 33l 14.)
QUESTTON
Do Mayor Messina, Vice-Mayor Patterson, and Councilmember Campbell have
potential conflicts of interest that would prohibit them from participating in a
governmental decision regarding the City's consideration of the Downtown Mixed Use
District?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Mayor Messina, Vice-Mayor Patterson, and Councilmember Campbell have
potential conflicts of interest that would prohibit them from participating in a
governmental decision regarding the City's consideration of the Downtown Mixed Use
District.
I Govemment Code sections 81000 - gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. All statutory references are to the Government Code
unless otherwise indicated. All regulatory references are to Title 2 of the Code of Regulation unless
otherwise indicated.
-:,.:, - )
,.9'-E


File No. ,4.-05-041
Page No. 2
FACTS & AIYALYSIS
In the McLaughlin Advice LetterNo. A-05-013(a) weprovided you advice
regarding whether there was a potential conflict of interest for Mayor Messina and
Councilmember Campbell in participating in goveflrmental decisions regarding the
Downtown Stoeetscape Plan. The potential conflict was related to their ieal property
interests within 500 feet of the boundaries of the project. The same real property inierests
are involved here with respect to theirproximityto the Downtown Mixed Use District
and the govemmental decision involved therein. Additional, Vice-Mayor patterson also
owns property within 500 feet of the boundaries of the district.
Your current request reviews the eight-step conflict ofinterest analysis, as set
forth in our last letter, as applied to the potential conflicts of interest for three of the
public officials involved in this decision. For the reasons stated in Mcl^aughlin, supra,
and as outlined in your request for advice herein, each of the officials has ipotential
conflict of interest based on the proximity of their property (within 500 feeti to the
boundaries of the project being affected by the governmental decision. As you noted, in
your incoming request, since Vice-Major Patterson's property "is almost beyond 500
feet," there may be factors that rebut the presumption that the affect on her property is
material. Please keep in mind, however, that any effect, even one-penny, is prJsumed to
be material. (Regulati on 187 05.2(aXl ).)
If you have any other questions regarding this matter,please contact me at
(916)322-s660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Division
